final_text:
We are given:

\[
\text{FeO} + \text{CO} = \text{Fe} + \text{CO}_2
\]
\[
\Delta G^\circ = 8120 \, \text{J at } 1173 \, \text{K}
\]

The equilibrium constant \(K\) is related to \(\Delta G^\circ\) by:

\[
\Delta G^\circ = -RT \ln K
\]

So:

\[
\ln K = -\frac{\Delta G^\circ}{RT}
\]

Using \(R = 8.314 \, \text{J/mol·K}\):

\[
\ln K = -\frac{8120}{8.314 \times 1173}
\]
\[
\ln K = -\frac{8120}{9752.322} \approx -0.8327
\]
\[
K \approx e^{-0.8327} \approx 0.435
\]

For the reaction,  
\[
K = \frac{p_{\text{CO}_2}}{p_{\text{CO}}}
\]
Thus:
\[
\frac{p_{\text{CO}}}{p_{\text{CO}_2}} = \frac{1}{K} \approx \frac{1}{0.435} \approx 2.3
\]

The correct option is (D).

[ANSWER]D[/ANSWER]
